load_bearer_review.verdict: Plan design is correct. FALLBACK_MANIFEST expansion from 1→11 capabilities is necessary to avoid breaking every external plugin.

key_findings_integrated.finding: PluginInput adding fetch/env is additive — optional fields won't break destructuring
key_findings_integrated.status: confirmed — plan already treats as additive

key_findings_integrated.finding: Built-in plugins (codex, copilot, xai, DO) use client.auth.set() and raw fetch() heavily
key_findings_integrated.status: safe — trust:built-in bypasses all capability checks per enforcer.ts:28

key_findings_integrated.finding: Shell $ is never used by any built-in plugin
key_findings_integrated.status: surprising but confirms shell proxy won't break internals

key_findings_integrated.finding: 23+ test files touch plugin system. PluginInput literal construction in tests may need fetch/env fields.
key_findings_integrated.status: test strategy in plan covers this. Fields should be optional to minimize test churn.

key_findings_integrated.finding: External plugins currently have unrestricted SDK access. FALLBACK_MANIFEST with only tool.register would break all of them.
key_findings_integrated.status: mitigated — plan expands FALLBACK_MANIFEST to 11 capabilities including sdk.access, network.request, config.read, filesystem.read, etc.
